commands.review_summary:
The PR moves core `doctor --lint` health execution to ordered doctor contributions, keeps extension/plugin checks on the shared registry path, updates focused tests and docs, and removes the temporary conversion-plan ledger.

PR surface: Source -206, Tests -51, Docs +2. Total -255 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a PR refactor/ordering change rather than a standalone bug report. Source and tests show the intended path, but the PR still lacks real after-patch CLI output from `openclaw doctor`.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer review plus contributor real behavior proof; no narrow code repair emerged from this review.

target.title: Keep core doctor health in contribution order
target.body:
## Summary

This PR integrates the existing structured health registry with the ordered core doctor checks. The important model is now explicit:

- core doctor checks stay owned by `resolveDoctorHealthContributions()` / `createDoctorHealthContribution(...)`, which remains the source of truth for `openclaw doctor` and `openclaw doctor --fix` ordering
- core contributions already carry `healthCheckIds`; this PR makes those IDs the bridge for running upgraded core structured checks in the same doctor slot instead of through a separate core registry pass
- `doctor --lint` runs structured core checks in contribution order first, then appends non-core bundled/plugin registry checks
- `doctor --fix` remains positional for core checks; the broad structured repair bridge is reserved for extension/plugin checks rather than becoming a second core repair runner
- the temporary doctor health conversion-plan ledger is removed because contribution ordering and contribution/core-check tests now own the invariant directly

That gives us the simple core-upgrade path we wanted: keep the existing doctor repair behavior in place, add structured lint/dry-run findings beside it, and only move mutation when that specific core contribution is ready.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` (6 files, 50 tests)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean after removing the conversion ledger
- `codex review --base origin/main` clean

## Updated In-Place Upgrade Stack

These are the doctor PRs already updated to take advantage of this model:

- #85566 - shell completion: structured lint health is owned by the existing `doctor:shell-completion` contribution via `healthCheckIds: ["core/doctor/shell-completion"]`.
- #84290 - UI freshness: structured lint/dry-run effects report missing/stale Control UI assets, while real build/rebuild stays on the existing doctor UI freshness path.
- #84366 - session locks: structured lint/dry-run effects report stale lock cleanup, while real cleanup stays on the existing session-lock doctor path.

The remaining older doctor repair PRs should be rebased onto this PR before they are presented as part of the same in-place upgrade series.
target.author: giodl73-repo
